ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Kutse wĩ awũ babi wĩ, ade kutse kpa lɛmɛ awũ babi kpa a.
translated: If I go to my father, then you also go to your father.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Nde sũ adã sukuluku kĩ sideĩ nɛ awaɛ̃ linebi kamɛ lɛmɛ aámɔ luvokũ a kĩ lideĩ nɛ nɔ mɔawɛ linebi kamɛ a?
translated: Are you going to school to teach, and what work do you do with the children?

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Mitawɛ akũ kana fɔtsa miyi akũ nɛ tite kĩĩ akũ, awã kĩ fupuka mɔ nkandɔlɔ aabula foe kade, ade bayekpanɛ lɛmɛ abubuli kɔla kawo kaye foe a.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Nda kɔa lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa edo nɛ yĩ awɛ. Odima linyína Obi a mbɔɛntɛ Ɔlɛga a odi, ade odima lɛmɛ linyína Ɔlɛga a mbɔɛntɛ Obi a odi, mɔ bikĩ Obi a awɛ kĩ yanyɛ ye ɛtsa a.
translated: The matter of Lɛtsa is Lɛtsa's child. The father of Obi and the mother of Olɛga, and the other Odima, father of Olɛga and mother of Obi, they are saying that Obi's child is like that.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Kĩ Yesu nɛ edzi nɛ Kugua Futse Ɔtɔ a akũ a, ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a bɛla bɔɛɛ bɛya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃ bivia ye kĩ, “Lɔkɔ lɔwɔdabe fɔtsa kĩĩ aaya nɛ foe kamɛ. Bi boe lɛtsa a kĩ laanya nɔ kaya mɔ katinya kamɛ kawũna a nsɛntsɛlɛni.”
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, “Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature. Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.”

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Ntɛ odi sama nɔ nɛ fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ ni, bɔa mbɔdi milɔla mi kayite nɛ lɔkɔ a kĩ lideĩ fɔmɔ baavɛ kalɛ a. Ntɛ nnɛ́ aye mɔ miwo fɔlɛtsa kadzɛkɔ̃ a, oti a aakɔa nɔ edo nɛ fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ awɛ. Fɔlɛtsa ɔdzɛnɛ a lɛmɛ aakɔa nɔ ɛkpa polisifɔ ade baado nɔ nɛ kɔla a.
translated: If a person does not do the work of the elders, he will not find peace in his heart. But the child who does good will find peace in his heart. If someone does not do the work of the elders, he will not be able to rest from the work of the ancestors. The work of the ancestors is not easy; it is like a heavy burden that one must carry.

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Foesũ Yosef kpã obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma bimuniki bɛya nɛ Israel tite akũ.
translated: Joseph dreamed a dream and told his brothers, which made them feel envy, and Israel his father rebuked him.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: bibi be kĩ, “Milɛ kĩ, ‘Lɛnyɛ a kĩ bɔwa tida a ade ye fɔtsa bakɔlɛnɛ a yaye ɔnɔkpa a bɛdzakũmɛna a.’
translated: Bibi said to me, "Milɛ said to me, 'Lɛnyɛ told me today that he has eaten the food that the child has well prepared.'"

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Bio, odima lákɔa waĩ vɔɛ̃ kako nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ kɔɛ kamɛ, kitonɛkĩ waĩ vɔɛ̃ aabɛ̃ kuvũ kɔɛ a, lɔkɔa waĩ a aawula, kuvũ a lɛmɛ aabu kade. Mbom bakɔa waĩ vɔɛ̃ kako nɛ ɔyaɛ ovoli kuvũ vɔɛ̃ kamɛ.” Fɔlɛtsa Kavia Nɛ Kawɛya Luwi a Akũ.
translated: Say, the word of the Lord came to them, and they were healed by his hands completely. He said to them, "Go to the water and wash and be clean." So they did as the Lord commanded them.

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yesu tsã emini nɛ Galilea tite flee akũ ɛkatsa fɔtsa nɛ Yudafɔ fɔtsɛnakɔ̃ a. Ede lɛtsã nɛ Kalɛ Wĩ akũ nɛ Baguma lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a akũ, ade ɛtsa fɔsɔ ahɔlɔ ahɔlɔ flee a.
translated: Jesus went to Galilee to teach the people and healed many sick people from Judea. Then he left Capernaum and went to the region of Baguma, and then he came to the Sea of Galilee and taught the people.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Fɔmɔ aana kutse libi wĩ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ana kutse wĩ, ade ntɛ odeĩ kutse kpa ni, kutse libi kpa aana. Babi a kĩ kutse awũ a bakɔa kanyi kutse a odu.
translated: The elder's word is life to me, and my mother's word to me, the child does not reject the community's word, the life-giving word of the community. The father has my word as a treasure, the wise one has the word of God.

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Foesũ lɛtsa nyɛ lɛtsa a kĩ miawɛ kĩ bati bɛbã bɛkɛna bɛkpa mi ni, mi malo mikɛna foe lɔmɔ mikpa be. Ade fukĩĩ Mose mbla a mɔ Baguma onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ fɔlɛtsa a atsa a. Mitsã Onyukpɛ̃ Manɛwa a Kamɛ Miwo.
translated: The commandment of the commandment which I command you today, you shall not add to it nor take away from it, so that you may keep the commandments of the Lord your God which I command you. And Moses spoke the word of the Lord to the heavens and to his people. Then the Lord Manasseh and King Miwo.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: Ĩya kĩ makɛna ‘babinyole biyidza bɛdza nɛ balɛga akũ, babitsole liyidza bɛdza nɛ bama akũ, babi batsɔ̃ biyidza bɛdza nɛ be nyina amɛ akũ.
translated: This is the story of the babinyole, who are strong; the babitsole, who are wise; and the batsɔ̃, who are accompanied by their children.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ade ntɛ mi bakɔba odi miado onukpɛ̃ ni, litsa mitɛnɛ kakɛna? Amamufɔ malo akɛna foe lɔmɔ!
translated: Ade is my father's name, what is your name? May you be well and peaceful!

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Adeke Yudafɔ mbla batsanɛ badi bi be akũ kĩ, “Onyole kĩĩ alɛ fubusuotsa!”
translated: Adeke Judah went to find Badi and found him there, saying, "Onyole, hurry up!"

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: ade ebi ye kĩ, “Yidza, kakpã obisɛ̃ a mɔ ɔma mimuniki minaa Israelfɔ tite akũ, kitonɛkĩ bikĩ bɛwɛ kĩ bakɔ obisɛ̃ a ku.”
translated: Go, tell the children of Israel, "I am the Lord who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan to be your God."

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Midã kɔvɔlabi, kɔádu funitsã, kɔákpɔta funitsã kako nɛ tili kamɛ, lɛmɛ mi Ɔlɛga nɛ ode a adɔɔla koe! Mbɔɛɛ mitátɛ kɔya miba kɔvɔlabi?
translated: When the bird flew, the child cried, the dog barked, and the tree fell, then Olega spoke to the people! Why did you do that, my friend?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Fɔmɔ aana kutse libi wĩ ni, kɛnɛ kĩ ana kutse wĩ, ade ntɛ odeĩ kutse kpa ni, kutse libi kpa aana. Babi a kĩ kutse awũ a bakɔa kanyi kutse a odu.
translated: The child of the woman is the heart of the family, and the one who is the heart of the family, when he dies, the family's heart is broken. The father who was the heart of the village has died.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Bati pii aamuniki nɛ be katɛkado liti. Baanyɛ be akũ akũ abɔa, bikesĩ akũ lɛmɛ.
translated: Translation not possible due to unrecognized language: Tuwuli

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Lɛmɛ Petro sĩ nɛ bati a flee anɛ ɛlɛ kĩ, “Ĩnyina lɛtsama nɛ lɛtsa a kĩ alɛ a akũ.”
translated: Then Peter, seeing this, fled and said, "Lord, you have left me and forsaken me."

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Banɛkunɛ bakɔasia! Fɔɔdabe bɛ̃ kɔya? Sika ngbã a mbɔɛɛ Asɔli Kɔla a kĩ kɔfɔ̃ sika a akũ kpalɛ a?
translated: Banɛkunɛ has arrived! Where are you going, Fɔɔdabe? Are you taking the money and leaving Asɔli Kɔla to hide the money and return?

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ke liti a, ɛnyɛ be osi kĩ bɛnaa Betlehem mɔ ɔlɛdo kĩ, “Minaawɛ obisɛ̃ a kawɛ wĩ. Ntɛ mimɔ́ ye ni, mifɔ̃ ĩnyi lɔkɔa yĩ malo nnaasum ye.”
translated: And he said, behold, I have established a house in Bethlehem; I will build a house for my name. If I build it, I will establish my name there forever.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ade ntɛ mi bakɔba odi miado onukpɛ̃ ni, litsa mitɛnɛ kakɛna? Amamufɔ malo akɛna foe lɔmɔ!
translated: Where have my father and mother gone? Are you all asking me? Amamufɔ's children ask for God!

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Fɔvã onukpɛ̃ banyɛnɛ aanɛ bɛkpã bati pii bɛyɛla osi.
translated: He went to his child and they ate a lot of food there.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Foesũ bati a kamɛ odi bi ye kĩ, “Dã, ama mɔ awaɛ̃nyole amɛ dzɛ̃ nɛ kanya bawɛ kĩ balɛ kɔdzɛla mɔ nɔ.”
translated: "Foesũ said to them when he saw them, 'Look, I have told you that when you enter the house, they will prepare your way.'"

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Fɔtsa odunɛ a nyɛ oti kĩ ɛtsɛ̃ yalɛ Baguma kalɛ.
translated: Fɔtsa odunɛ is in Oti and in Baguma kalɛ.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ke liti a, ye malo ede ɛnaa ɔtɔ kalete ɛnabɔa mpaɛ.
translated: And he came, he found money there when he started to go to the house.

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Nɛ foesũ nvila, ade nnakɔa nɔ sika a nnabila nɛ tite kamɛ a. Amɔ nɔ fɔtsa nɛ awĩ.’
translated: The child went to the farm, then he searched for money and found the white chicken. But he sold the goat.

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “Nnɛ́ odi nyɛ odi kĩ yavɛ yĩ kĩ, ‘Boe Ɔlate! Boe Ɔlate!’ aanaa ode lɛgãkanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ, mbɔɛntɛ bikĩ bakɛna nɛ Nda kawɛ akũ a odi aanaa awã.
translated: When the child is in distress, they cry out, "Help, Ɔlate! Help, Ɔlate!" but the ancestors who went before them are not there, and Nda has abandoned his people and their children.

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Bike kasɔã kɔkɔɛ nɛ futse tɔbɔlɛ akũ kĩ baatsua. Kutse nyɛ kutse kĩ kɔáwũ babi wĩ ni, baakpeli koe bɛta bido nɛ fi luwotu kamɛ.
translated: Bike rides the bicycle and carries them to Tɔbɔlɛ to Baatsua. Kutse is the child of Kutse in Ni, and Baakpeli is in the big house where they live in Kamɛ.

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Ade ntɛ bɔlɛ kĩ, ‘Eto bati sɛkɛ̃’ ni, bɔavila lɛtsa a kĩ bati a aakɛna, kitonɛkĩ be flee bɛtɛ bido kĩ Yohanes nyɛ Baguma onukpɛ̃ ɔnyɛnɛ.”
translated: The Word was with God, and the Word was true, he spoke to the people the words he heard, and John the Baptist, sent by God, came to testify about the light.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Kĩ Yesu sila ɛnɛ́ eto mui a kamɛ a, ɛmɔ kĩ odetɔ́ kamɛ binya ade Hũhũ a ka eko nɛ ye akũ ndɛ ayekĩ mblɔduma aka a.
translated: For Jesus taught them the way we believe in him, and he loved the world so much that he gave his only Son so that all who believe in him may not perish but have eternal life.

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: “Ye obi a kpa mbuayɛ kĩ, ‘Nláanaa,’ lɛmɛ ke liti a, fuwo ye kalɔɛ ade ɛnaa kɔba a.
translated: He called out to Mbuayɛ, "Nláanaa," and told his child, then he went to Kalɔɛ to fetch Kɔbaa there.

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Ade ntɛ odi tani Asɔli Kɔla a ni, ɛtani koe mɔ Baguma a kĩ edzi nɛ koe kamɛ a.
translated: Ade is the child of Asɔli Kɔla, and he went to Baguma's house and took his wealth.

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Kĩ bɛkadã ye a, Yesu akũ dani nɛ be anɛ kamɛ. Ye anɛ kamɛ kata ndɛ suwa aye, ade ye tɛtadiɛ lɛmɛ kɛna fitaa ndɛ ɔhaĩ aye a.
translated: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with Jesus, and the Word was God. The Word was with Jesus in the beginning. All things were made through him, and without him nothing was made that was made.

ref: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
text: Nnɛ́ bawaɛ̃tsole lɛmɛ deĩ nɛ boe kamɛ nɛ awĩ a? Ndia etomɛna fɔtsa kĩĩ bamɛ flee?”
translated: Did you come here and then leave again? Why did you flee from there?

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: “Ade bayitsɔ̃ ewua a yidza bɛtɛna be tɛsɛdza a.
translated: Ade and his people went to Betɛna to do something.

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Nɛ foesũ nvila, ade nnakɔa nɔ sika a nnabila nɛ tite kamɛ a. Amɔ nɔ fɔtsa nɛ awĩ.’
translated: The child went to the farm. After searching for money, he found it under the stone. But he took the snake.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Bati kɔdabu a wũni nɛ be akũ kĩ bɛfɔ̃ fɔwɔla kabɔa, lɛmɛ bɛbɔa fɔwɔla a kɔɔba bɛba bɛlɛ kĩ, “Boe Ɔlate, Dawid Obi, mɔ́ boe kɔnya ni!”
translated: When the elders realized that he was not in the house where he was staying, they went to the place where he was born and said, “Greetings, Elder David Obi, come and see for yourself!”

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Kĩ Yesu naa Petro tɔtɔ kamɛ a, ɛmɔ kĩ Petro nyina waa nɛ ɔkɛna akũ yasɔ atilili.
translated: Jesus said to Peter, "Do you love me?" and Peter answered him, "You know that I love you," and he said to him, "Feed my lambs."

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: Ɛkalɛ nɛ awã kĩ, “Lɔkɔ wĩ a wo ɔtɔ ade Baguma lɛgãkanya lɔkɔ a fuã. Mimuniki nɛ mi tɔkpa liti lɔkɔa mitɛ Kalɛ Wĩ a mido!”
translated: Ekalɛ and the word says, "He said to them, 'Go to Baguma's house and burn it down. Truly, you will see the people of the house perish by the word of Ekalɛ Wĩ!'"

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: “Foesũ baanamɔ bati kĩĩ lubokamɔ a kĩ lɛlá kawũna a kamɛ, lɛmɛ bati wĩ a aanaa nkpã kĩ ɛlá kawũna a kamɛ.”
translated: The strangers brought them food for the child at home, and they are eating yam with the child at home.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: kitonɛkĩ nnɛ́ mi aalɛ kɔdzɛla, mbom mi Ɔlɛga Hũhũ a aalɛ kɔdzɛla ɛtsãmɛna mi akũ.
translated: They began, we are working the task, but we, Olega Huhu, have finished working it.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Kitonɛkĩ odi nyɛ odi kĩ edeĩ fɔtsa ni, baakɔa fɔtsa pii bibu ye, ade yaana foe fublusu a. Lɛmɛ oti a kĩ ɛlá lɛtsama ni, foe kabii a kĩ edeĩ a malo baatɛ foe nɛ ye awɛ.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Mabi mi kĩ nláabuki mmɔa waĩ kĩĩ kɛnaawo luwi a kĩ bɔmɔ mi bɔmɔa waĩ vɔɛ̃ nɛ Nda Lɛgã kanyakɔ̃ a kamɛ.”
translated: I spoke the word to them in the water, and I came when they went to Nda Lega who was called and is.
